question: [File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.multicall] Can an unprivileged attacker who holds a valid signed Authorization struct under preconditions where the authorizer has previously authorized the attacker on Midnight trigger setIsAuthorized to grant themselves operator rights, then compose a Midnight.multicall(calls) with calls.length set to an arbitrarily large value (e.g., 10_000 delegatecall entries each invoking touchMarket with 128 collateralParams), violating the invariant that a single transaction cannot consume unbounded gas through multicall composition, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS of any caller who must share the same block gas limit? Proof idea: fuzz calls.length from 1 to block.gaslimit / 21000 and assert gas consumed grows linearly without bound.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged attacker who obtains authorization via a signed Authorization struct under preconditions where authorization.isAuthorized=true and the victim has debt trigger setIsAuthorized to become an authorized operator, then call supplyCollateral for the victim across all MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) slots with dust amounts of 16 distinct collateral tokens, violating the invariant that a borrower's health check cost is bounded by a constant, causing scoped impact: every subsequent isHealthy, take (seller path), withdrawCollateral, and liquidate call for the victim now iterates 16 oracle calls, griefing liquidators and the victim? Proof idea: measure gas of isHealthy before and after activating 16 collaterals; assert gas delta scales with collateral count.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (collateralBitmap while-loop)] Can an unprivileged borrower who self-activates 16 collateral slots (MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER) with dust amounts under preconditions where each collateral's oracle is a maximally expensive view function trigger liquidate, causing the while (_collateralBitmap != 0) loop to call IOracle.price() 16 times per liquidation, violating the invariant that liquidation must remain callable within a reasonable gas budget, causing scoped impact: liquidation of the borrower becomes economically unviable or reverts due to out-of-gas when oracle calls are expensive? Proof idea: deploy 16 oracles each consuming ~50k gas; assert liquidate reverts or costs >5M gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy (collateralBitmap while-loop) called from take] Can an unprivileged seller who activates 16 collateral slots under preconditions where each oracle is a gas-heavy contract trigger take (offer.buy=true, seller=victim), causing isHealthy to iterate 16 oracle calls at line 950-957 of Midnight.sol, violating the invariant that take gas cost is bounded independently of collateral count, causing scoped impact: takers are griefed with unexpectedly high gas costs when taking against sellers with many activated collaterals? Proof idea: fuzz collateral count from 1 to 16 and assert take gas grows proportionally.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.touchMarket (collateralParams for-loop)] Can an unprivileged market creator under preconditions where market.collateralParams.length == MAX_COLLATERALS (128) trigger touchMarket with 128 valid, sorted CollateralParams entries, causing the for-loop at line 762 of Midnight.sol to iterate 128 times checking token ordering and LLTV validity, violating the invariant that market creation gas is bounded by a small constant, causing scoped impact: market creation transactions consume disproportionate gas, and any function that calls touchMarket (take, withdraw, repay, supplyCollateral, withdrawCollateral, liquidate) on an uncreated market pays this cost? Proof idea: deploy market with 1 vs 128 collaterals and measure gas delta.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.flashLoan (tokens loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where the attacker controls the callback contract trigger flashLoan with tokens.length and assets.length set to an arbitrarily large value (no upper bound check exists), causing two for-loops (lines 742-744 and 749-751 of Midnight.sol) to each iterate over the full array, violating the invariant that flashLoan gas cost is bounded, causing scoped impact: block gas exhaustion or DoS of the flashLoan function for legitimate users sharing the block? Proof idea: fuzz tokens.length from 1 to 1000 and assert gas grows linearly without protocol-enforced cap.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (domain separator uses block.chainid)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions of a hard fork that changes block.chainid trigger setIsAuthorized with a pre-fork signature, observing that EcrecoverAuthorizer computes domainSeparator = keccak256(abi.encode(EIP712_DOMAIN_TYPEHASH, block.chainid, address(this))) using the live block.chainid while Midnight.sol uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID for market IDs, violating the invariant that authorization domain and market domain are consistent, causing scoped impact: post-fork, pre-fork signatures become invalid in EcrecoverAuthorizer but market IDs remain valid, creating a mismatch that could strand authorized operators or allow replay on the minority chain? Proof idea: simulate chainid change and assert setIsAuthorized reverts on pre-fork signatures while Midnight market state is intact.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (nonce race condition)] Can an unprivileged front-runner under preconditions where a victim has broadcast a signed Authorization with nonce N trigger setIsAuthorized with the same Authorization and Signature before the victim's transaction, consuming nonce N and causing the victim's transaction to revert with InvalidNonce(), violating the invariant that a signed authorization can be submitted by its intended submitter, causing scoped impact: griefing of gasless authorization flows where the victim must re-sign with nonce N+1? Proof idea: mempool-watch for Authorization structs and front-run with identical calldata; assert victim tx reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (authorized-by-authorizer signer path)] Can an unprivileged attacker who is already authorized by the authorizer on Midnight under preconditions where IMidnight(MIDNIGHT).isAuthorized(authorization.authorizer, signer) == true trigger setIsAuthorized with a signature from the attacker (not the authorizer), granting or revoking authorization for any address on behalf of the authorizer, violating the invariant that only the authorizer or their explicitly intended delegate can modify authorization state, causing scoped impact: an authorized operator can silently deauthorize other operators or authorize new ones, escalating control? Proof idea: authorize attacker A on Midnight; have A sign an Authorization revoking operator B; call setIsAuthorized; assert B is deauthorized.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.setIsAuthorized (authorization chain)] Can an unprivileged attacker who is authorized by victim A under preconditions where isAuthorized[A][attacker]=true trigger setIsAuthorized on EcrecoverAuthorizer to authorize attacker-controlled address B on behalf of A, then have B authorize C, creating an unbounded authorization chain, violating the invariant that authorization delegation is a single-hop relationship, causing scoped impact: attacker can propagate full Midnight control to arbitrary addresses without the original authorizer's knowledge? Proof idea: build a 3-hop authorization chain and assert each hop can call setIsAuthorized on Midnight on behalf of the original authorizer.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (isAuthorized=false griefing)] Can an unprivileged attacker who is authorized by victim A under preconditions where isAuthorized[A][attacker]=true trigger setIsAuthorized with authorization.isAuthorized=false and authorization.authorized=<victim's critical operator>, revoking the victim's operator without their consent, violating the invariant that authorization revocation requires the authorizer's explicit action, causing scoped impact: victim's automated operator (e.g., a liquidation bot or bundler) is deauthorized mid-operation, freezing their position management? Proof idea: authorize attacker; have attacker sign Authorization{isAuthorized:false, authorized:victim_operator}; call setIsAuthorized; assert victim_operator can no longer act.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where the attacker has a valid signed Authorization trigger a Midnight.multicall that batches (1) setIsAuthorized to authorize attacker, (2) supplyCollateral activating 16 collateral slots for a victim, (3) repeated touchMarket calls for 128-collateral markets, all in a single transaction, violating the invariant that multicall cannot be used to compose gas-amplified state changes, causing scoped impact: single transaction exhausts block gas budget, griefing other users in the same block? Proof idea: construct multicall with N=100 touchMarket(128-collateral market) calls and measure total gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral (takes loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker who controls the takes array under preconditions where the attacker is authorized as taker trigger buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral with takes.length set to an arbitrarily large value (no upper bound), causing the for-loop at line 71 of MidnightBundles.sol to iterate over all entries (each calling ConsumableUnitsLib.consumableUnits and attempting IMidnight.take), violating the invariant that bundler gas cost is bounded, causing scoped impact: gas griefing of the msg.sender who pays for all iterations including failed take attempts? Proof idea: fuzz takes.length from 1 to 1000 with all-failing offers and assert gas grows linearly.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral (collateralWithdrawals loop)] Can an unprivileged caller under preconditions where onBehalf has authorized the caller trigger repayAndWithdrawCollateral with collateralWithdrawals.length set to an arbitrarily large value, causing the for-loop at line 336 of MidnightBundles.sol to call withdrawCollateral for each entry (each invoking isHealthy with up to 16 oracle calls), violating the invariant that repay+withdraw gas is bounded, causing scoped impact: gas griefing of the caller or DoS of the repay flow? Proof idea: fuzz collateralWithdrawals.length and assert gas grows with both array length and activated collateral count.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget (collateralSupplies loop)] Can an unprivileged attacker who is authorized as taker under preconditions where collateralSupplies.length is unbounded trigger supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with a large collateralSupplies array, causing the for-loop at line 134 of MidnightBundles.sol to call pullToken + forceApproveMax + supplyCollateral for each entry, violating the invariant that bundler collateral supply gas is bounded, causing scoped impact: gas griefing of msg.sender and potential activation of all 16 collateral slots in a single bundler call? Proof idea: supply 16 distinct collateral tokens via bundler and assert gas cost and collateralBitmap state.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (while-loop + bad debt realization)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where a borrower has 16 activated collaterals and the market has lossFactor near type(uint128).max trigger liquidate with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 (bad debt only path), causing the while (_collateralBitmap != 0) loop to iterate 16 times calling IOracle.price() for each collateral even though no seizure occurs, violating the invariant that zero-seizure bad debt realization should not require full oracle traversal, causing scoped impact: bad debt realization becomes prohibitively expensive when borrower has many collaterals? Proof idea: create borrower with 16 collaterals, set all to dust, call liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) and measure gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.isHealthy (while-loop) called from withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower who has activated 16 collateral slots under preconditions where each oracle is a gas-heavy contract trigger withdrawCollateral for a single collateral, causing isHealthy at line 568 of Midnight.sol to iterate all 16 active collaterals via the while-loop, violating the invariant that withdrawCollateral gas is proportional to the withdrawn collateral only, causing scoped impact: borrowers with many collaterals pay disproportionate gas for each withdrawal? Proof idea: activate 1 vs 16 collaterals and compare withdrawCollateral gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (ecrecover address(0) check)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where signature.v, r, s are crafted to produce ecrecover returning address(0) trigger setIsAuthorized with authorization.authorizer=address(0), bypassing the signer != address(0) check at line 32 of EcrecoverAuthorizer.sol only if authorizer is also address(0), violating the invariant that invalid signatures must always revert, causing scoped impact: if address(0) has any Midnight authorization state, it could be manipulated? Proof idea: craft (v,r,s) that makes ecrecover return address(0); call setIsAuthorized with authorizer=address(0); assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (deadline == block.timestamp boundary)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where authorization.deadline == block.timestamp trigger setIsAuthorized, observing that the check is block.timestamp <= authorization.deadline (inclusive), and then front-run the authorizer's intended revocation by submitting the authorization in the same block as the deadline, violating the invariant that deadline-expired authorizations cannot be submitted, causing scoped impact: authorization is granted in the same block as its deadline, potentially after the authorizer expected it to be invalid? Proof idea: set deadline=block.timestamp; submit in same block; assert success.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (signature malleability)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where a valid (v, r, s) signature exists trigger setIsAuthorized with the malleable counterpart (v' = 27+28-v, s' = secp256k1_order - s), observing that ecrecover accepts both forms and Solidity 0.8.34 does not enforce low-s, violating the invariant that each signed authorization corresponds to exactly one valid submission, causing scoped impact: the same authorization intent can be submitted twice if the nonce check is bypassed (it is not, but malleability allows a different submitter to front-run with the alternate form)? Proof idea: compute malleable (v',r,s') from valid sig; assert both produce same signer; assert nonce prevents double-use.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized (cross-contract replay)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where two EcrecoverAuthorizer instances are deployed (e.g., for different Midnight deployments) trigger setIsAuthorized on the second instance using a signature intended for the first, observing that the domain separator includes address(this) so replay is prevented, but verifying that the AUTHORIZATION_TYPEHASH constant is identical across both instances, violating the invariant that signatures are bound to a specific EcrecoverAuthorizer instance, causing scoped impact: if domain separator computation has any flaw, cross-instance replay could grant unintended authorization? Proof idea: deploy two EcrecoverAuthorizer instances; sign for instance A; attempt replay on instance B; assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall (delegatecall loop, no length bound)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where the attacker calls Midnight.multicall directly trigger multicall with calls.length = N where N is chosen to consume exactly block.gaslimit gas, causing the for-loop at line 212 of Midnight.sol to delegatecall N times with each call being a no-op (e.g., empty calldata that hits the fallback), violating the invariant that multicall cannot be used as a gas amplifier, causing scoped impact: block stuffing attack that prevents other transactions from being included? Proof idea: binary search N such that multicall(N no-ops) consumes >99% of block gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.multicall + Midnight.touchMarket (128-collateral market)] Can an unprivileged attacker under preconditions where the attacker constructs a Market struct with 128 valid sorted collateralParams trigger multicall([touchMarket(128-collateral-market), touchMarket(128-collateral-market), ...]) with M repetitions, observing that touchMarket is idempotent after first call but still executes the full for-loop on first call, violating the invariant that market creation cost is paid once, causing scoped impact: attacker can force a single transaction to pay 128 * M collateral validation iterations? Proof idea: call multicall with 10 distinct 128-collateral markets and measure gas.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: Midnight.liquidate (collateralBitmap loop, liquidatedCollatPrice=0 path)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under preconditions where the borrower has 16 activated collaterals and collateralIndex is not in the bitmap trigger liquidate with repaidUnits > 0, causing the while-loop to set liquidatedCollatPrice=0 (since i==collateralIndex is never true), and then repaidUnits computation at line 652 to divide by liquidatedCollatPrice=0, violating the invariant that liquidation with repaidUnits input must not revert due to division by zero when collateralIndex is not activated, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly, leaving unhealthy positions unliquidatable? Proof idea: liquidate with collateralIndex not in bitmap and repaidUnits>0; assert revert behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/interfaces/IEcrecoverAuthorizer.sol -> Scope: Medium Gas griefing or unbounded loop behavior in collateral lists, market creation, liquidation, health checks, or multicall composition'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer.setIsAuthorized + Midnight.supplyCollateral (collateral poisoning via authorized operator)] Can an unprivileged attacker who is authorized by victim under preconditions where victim has debt and fewer than 16 active collaterals trigger supplyCollateral for victim with 1 wei of a collateral token whose oracle always reverts, activating that collateral slot in victim's bitmap, violating the invariant that activated collateral oracles must not cause isHealthy/liquidate to revert, causing scoped impact: victim's position becomes permanently unliquidatable and withdrawCollateral reverts, freezing victim's funds? Proof idea: authorize attacker; attacker calls supplyCollateral(victim, reverting-oracle-collateral, 1); assert liquidate reverts.
